arguments.task: Research Datadog using official first-party sources. Open Datadog API docs, developer/authentication docs, pricing or plan/access pages, rate limit docs, and any official GitHub or MCP pages if linked. Extract: one-line description of Datadog, supported API auth methods, whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/admin-gated/partner-gated and why, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, whether an MCP server exists, buildability for an AI-agent toolkit today, main blocker if not ready_today, rate limit notes, and notable signals. Return exact supporting official URLs for each finding. Prefer docs.datadoghq.com, app.datadoghq.com, docs.datadoghq.com/account_management/api-app-keys, docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/rate-limits, pricing pages, and official GitHub pages. Do not use third-party blogs.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect Datadog official docs and extract evidence-backed app research fields.

raw_result.description: Observability and security platform with APIs for monitoring, logs, traces, incidents, security, and related operations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs state Datadog API requests use an API key and an application key; the application key is required for many read/full-access operations. Application keys are a Datadog-specific credential type, so it is marked as other in addition to api_key.
raw_result.gating_reason: Datadog has self-serve signup and a free tier/trial path on its pricing page rather than partner or admin gating.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API surface across metrics, logs, traces/APM, dashboards, monitors, incidents, service management, cloud cost, CI/CD, security, and other observability/security resources listed in the API reference.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Datadog has an official MCP server product/docs presence, but the exact implementation/repository details were not fully verified from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate limit docs describe standard rate-limit headers such as X-RateLimit-Limit and related headers. Metric and log submission endpoints are noted as not rate-limited for submission, event submissions are limited to 250,000 events per minute, and limits for other endpoints vary by endpoint.
raw_result.notable_signals: Public official API reference is extensive; authentication is clearly documented; Datadog publicly markets an MCP server for AI-agent/IDE use cases; pricing is self-serve with free/trial signals, which supports initial toolkit buildability.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Two points remain slightly ambiguous from the gathered evidence alone: whether the best access-tier classification should be self_serve_free versus self_serve_trial given product/package nuances, and the MCP details/repository were confirmed at the product/docs level but not fully validated at code/release level.

result.notable_signals: Public official API reference is extensive; authentication is clearly documented; Datadog publicly markets an MCP server for AI-agent/IDE use cases; pricing is self-serve with free/trial signals, which supports initial toolkit buildability.
